title: Cross build support for different architectures
body:
Currently one can build docker images for other architectures by registering entries using binfmt_misc and adding (or bind mounting) the appropriate static qemu usermode binaries in the build container.

Although I haven't tested it, I suspect you could accomplish this today by configuring binfmt_misc and running buildah from a container containing the static qemu usermode binary. 

It would be nice if there was a simple method for accomplishing this natively with buildah. 

For the short term being able to specify a "host container" to use for executing "native" commands would probably meet most of the use cases.

Longer term, full native workflow support that would allow you to:
- automatically configure binfmt_misc appropriately
- automatically bind mount the appropriate qemu usermode binary into the builder image as needed.
- specify multiple architectures when creating images
- use default builder images based on host OS and arches specified, but allow for overriding
- be able to run buildah commands in parallel for all arches, with a flag for specifying how many parallel processes to run at a time.
- be able to run "native" commands from within the appropriate builder container for each arch
- be able to use and generate manifest list images (this should be toggleable until all tooling supports manifest list images)

comments.body: @detiber have you looked any further into this?
@detiber we've had no traction on this one and have not heard from you or others requesting this.  I'm going to close this now and please reopen if you think the closure was hasty.

The approach described above is technically feasibly and often used for building embedded images.

I also use this approach within my personal project: https://www.get-edi.io/A-new-Approach-to-Operating-System-Image-Generation/
We would be very willing to look at and review patches to support this.  But it is not high on the priority list of the core developers at this time.  PRs welcomed.
